LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RICHARD LIBERMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AND HIS ALTERNATIVE REQUEST FOR CONSOLIDATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING RE-FILED BASED AT A LATER STAGE IN THESE CASES IF UNDER THE THEN-EXISTING CIRCUMSTANCES JUDICIAL ECONOMY WOULD BE ADVANCED BY CONSOLIDATION. THE CLAIMS IN THIS CASE AND THE PROBATE CASE ARE DIFFERENT. AN ELDER ABUSE ACT CLAIM IS INTENDED TO PROTECT A VULNERABLE PORTION OF THE POPULATION FROM ABUSE AND CUSTODIAL NEGLECT. (IN RE ESTATE OF DITO (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 791, 802.) IN THIS CASE THE PRIMARY RIGHT TO BE FREE OF ABUSE AND NEGLECT BELONGS TO THE DECEDENT. SIMILARLY, THE FRAUD CLAIM IN THIS CASE IS ALLEGED ON DECEDENT'S BEHALF AND SEEKS REDRESS FOR DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS WHICH ASSERTEDLY CAUSED DECEDENT TO SELL HER PROPERTY BELOW FAIR MARKET VALUE. ON THE OTHER HAND, THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE PROBATE CASE ARE BEING ASSERTED AS A MEANS OF INVALIDATING THE WILL AND ENHANCING PLAINTIFFS' OWN INTERESTS. THUS, EVEN THOUGH BOTH ACTIONS ARISE OUT OF THE SAME FACTS AND THERE IS OVERLAP BETWEEN THE TWO PROCEEDINGS, THE ASSERTED PRIMARY RIGHTS ARE DIFFERENT. AT THIS STAGE OF THE TWO CASES JUDICIAL ECONOMY DOES NOT SUPPORT THE REQUEST TO CONSOLIDATE. THAT MAY CHANGE LATER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARISSA L. WALTER, ESQ. of Albertson & Davidson LLP, Tel No. 760-804-2711 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. ANDREW ZABRONSKY, ESQ. of Evans, Latham & Campisi APLC, Tel No. 415-421-0288 appearing for Defendant Richard Michael Liberman.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT RICHARD LIBERMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendant's demurrer is overruled and his alternative request for consolidation is denied without prejudice to being re-filed based at a later stage in these cases if under the then-existing circumstances judicial economy would be advanced by consolidation. The claims in this case and the probate case are different. An Elder Abuse Act claim is intended to protect a vulnerable portion of the population from abuse and custodial neglect. (In re Estate of Dito (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 791, 802.) In this case the primary right to be free of abuse and neglect belongs to the decedent. Similarly, the fraud claim in this case is alleged on decedent's behalf and seeks redress for defendant's alleged misrepresentations which assertedly caused decedent to sell her property below fair market value. On the other hand, the allegations in the probate case are being asserted as a means of invalidating the will and enhancing plaintiffs' own interests. Thus, even though both actions arise out of the same facts and there is overlap between the two proceedings, the asserted primary rights are different. At this stage of the two cases judicial economy does not support the request to consolidate. That may change later. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
